notes.outcome_clarity: PARTIALLY PRESENT. Title indicates this is a documentation fix about IPv6 CIDR netmask note, but the actual changes are not shown in the spec. No description of what the 'confusing note' currently says or what it should say instead. Two developers would struggle to understand what exactly needs to be changed.
notes.error_states: ABSENT. No error conditions, edge cases, or failure modes mentioned. Not applicable to documentation change but worth noting.
notes.scope_boundaries: PARTIALLY PRESENT. Explicitly states this is a backport from PR #37029 with target release 1.13.x, but scope is vague—unclear if this affects only documentation file(s) or code examples, which Terraform versions are in scope, and whether changes apply to other related documentation.
notes.acceptance_criteria: ABSENT. No testable success criteria provided. No specification of what the corrected note should contain, no validation criteria for the fix, no before/after examples.
notes.data_contracts: ABSENT. Not applicable—this is a documentation change with no data structure modifications.
notes.dependency_context: PARTIALLY PRESENT. References original PRs (#37029, #33107) and mentions 'terraform' repository context, but does not specify which documentation file(s) are being modified or which code sections the documentation describes.
notes.behavioral_specificity: ABSENT. Very vague language: 'confusing note,' 'suggested corrections,' 'fixed indentation'—no concrete details about what is confusing, what corrections were suggested, or what indentation issues exist.
missing_critical_info: The actual diff or description of documentation changes
What the corrected note should say
Why the note is confusing (specific user impact)

title: Import references should not be able to reference the import target

acceptance_criteria_notes: No testable criteria given; only a vague reference to 'correct wording' without specifying what is incorrect or what correct looks like
dependency_context_notes: References issue #36763 and mentions 'CLI configuration page' but no file path or specific location provided
behavioral_specificity_notes: 'Correct wording' is entirely non-specific; no examples, before/after text, or concrete changes described
summary: This is a minimal documentation correction spec with almost no detail. It references an external issue (#36763) as the source of truth, mentions a target release, and marks itself as non-user-facing, but provides no actual description of what wording is wrong or what the correction should be. A developer would need to read issue #36763 to understand the work. The specification is essentially empty of actionable content.
